div.h1: Callback Re-entrancy: The Iterator Invalidation Problem

g.text: 0x04 | int | closing // MARK: set=1 during dispatch
Total: 40 bytes per slot | handlers[MAX_FDS] = 40 * 65536 = 2.5 MB
0x08 | uint32_t | events // for ADD and MOD only
Total: 12 bytes | MAX_PENDING_OPS=256 → 3 KB embedded in reactor_t
INVARIANT 1: dispatching==1 → all epoll_ctl calls enqueue to pending_ops[]
INVARIANT 2: handlers[fd].closing==1 → skip fd in ALL remaining iterations
INVARIANT 3: pending_ops[] applied ONLY after dispatching=0 (Phase 2)
INVARIANT 4: close(fd) called ONLY in Phase 2 — never inside dispatch loop
INVARIANT 5: free(conn) deferred via reactor_deregister — conn valid entire tick
reads data, decides fd=7 should close
OS recycles fd=7 for new connection!
OR: fd=7 is new conn → wrong handler fires
All epoll_ctl calls now enqueue to pending_ops[]
reads data, decides fd=7 should close
NO epoll_ctl called yet. NO close(). NO free().
No callback fires for closed fd=7
these invariants prevent the buggy path
